# HEAR V1 — Calibration Set Generation Plan

**Version**: 1.0
**Total items**: 50
**Source**: 100% synthetic, generated by Claude Opus 4.6
**Naming convention**: `NNN-{type}-{quality-hint}.md` (e.g., `007-spec-poor-reasoning.md`)

---

## Design principles

1. **Spread over the full quality range** — from deliberately poor (target 1–3) to excellent (target 8–10)
2. **Diverse types** — six types proportional to their value and frequency in real collaborative work
3. **Authentic voice** — items should read like real artifacts produced by actual agents, not artificial rubric-hitting exercises
4. **Differential targets** — many items should score high on some axes and low on others (this is how we test discriminant validity of the rubric)
5. **Edge cases included** — short artifacts, long verbose ones, confident-but-wrong ones, humble-but-right ones, etc.

---

## Distribution by type

| Type | Count | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| `decision` | 8 | Rare in practice but highest-value. Overrepresented for calibration. |
| `spec` | 10 | Common and high-value. Good for Reasoning Depth and Communication Clarity. |
| `pr` | 10 | Common. Good for Collaborative Intelligence and Communication Clarity. |
| `ticket` | 10 | Most common in practice. Short, focused, tests rubric on terse content. |
| `component` | 6 | Technical content, tests vocabulary and structure sensitivity. |
| `document` | 6 | Long-form, tests endurance and structure. |
| **Total** | **50** | |

---

## Distribution by quality tier

Not every item needs to target a specific tier globally — but the *set* must span the range.

| Tier | Description | Target count |
|---|---|---|
| 1 — Deliberately poor | Scores 1–3 on most axes | ~8 items |
| 2 — Below average | Scores 3–5 on most axes | ~10 items |
| 3 — Average | Scores 4–6 on most axes | ~12 items |
| 4 — Good | Scores 6–8 on most axes | ~10 items |
| 5 — Excellent | Scores 8–10 on most axes | ~5 items |
| M — Mixed | High on some axes, low on others | ~5 items |

Total: 8+10+12+10+5+5 = 50 ✓

The "Mixed" tier is especially important: it tests whether the rubric discriminates between axes. An artifact that scores 9 on Reasoning Depth but 3 on Communication Clarity validates that these two axes measure different things.

---

## The 50 items — targeted specs

Each item has a primary target axis (where the item is designed to hit a specific score range), an optional secondary target axis, and a descriptive filename. The generator (Opus) will produce natural artifacts matching these targets.

### Decisions (8 items)

| # | Filename | Primary target | Secondary target | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 001 | `001-decision-excellent-wisdom.md` | Decision Wisdom: 9-10 | Reasoning Depth: 8-9 | Full pre-mortem, trade-offs, reversibility, key uncertainty |
| 002 | `002-decision-good-balanced.md` | Decision Wisdom: 7-8 | — | Solid decision, few gaps |
| 003 | `003-decision-average.md` | Decision Wisdom: 5-6 | — | Lists considerations, doesn't anticipate consequences |
| 004 | `004-decision-poor-no-tradeoffs.md` | Decision Wisdom: 2-3 | — | Picks without acknowledging alternatives |
| 005 | `005-decision-verbose-shallow.md` | Decision Wisdom: 3-4 | Communication Clarity: 2-3 | Long, verbose, but actually shallow reasoning |
| 006 | `006-decision-mixed-quality.md` | Reasoning Depth: 8-9 | Communication Clarity: 2-3 | Deep thinking, terrible writing |
| 007 | `007-decision-humble-calibrated.md` | Self-Awareness: 8-9 | Decision Wisdom: 7-8 | Explicit uncertainty, asks for input |
| 008 | `008-decision-confident-wrong.md` | Self-Awareness: 1-2 | Decision Wisdom: 4-5 | Very confident, no uncertainty, logic gaps |

---

## Coverage matrix

The 50 items cover all 8 axes across the quality range:

| Axis | # items with explicit target on this axis | Range covered |
|---|---|---|
| Reasoning Depth | 12 | 1–10 |
| Decision Wisdom | 10 | 1–10 |
| Communication Clarity | 15 | 1–10 |
| Initiative Quality | 5 | 2–9 |
| Collaborative Intelligence | 8 | 2–10 |
| Self-Awareness & Calibration | 4 | 1–9 |
| Persona Coherence | 0 | N/A for single-artifact grading |
| Contextual Judgment | 6 | 2–10 |

**Note on Persona Coherence**: this axis requires longitudinal observation (comparing outputs across time) and cannot be graded from a single artifact. It will be marked "not gradable" on all 50 items. V2 will introduce a longitudinal calibration protocol.

**Note on Initiative Quality**: this axis requires behavior windows (24h slices). It is partially gradable from decisions and PRs that explicitly discuss when to act, but not from most artifacts. ~5 items will have a meaningful score; the rest are "not gradable".

---

## Generation process

Each item is generated by a single call to Claude Opus 4.6 with a prompt of the form:

> You are generating a calibration artifact for HEAR (a qualitative evaluation framework for AI agents). This item should be a realistic-looking **{type}** produced by an AI agent in a collaborative software development environment.
>
> **Primary target**: this item should score approximately **{primary_target_range}** on the axis **{primary_axis}** (full rubric definition below).
>
> **Secondary target** (if specified): this item should score approximately **{secondary_target_range}** on the axis **{secondary_axis}**.
>
> **Other axes**: let the other axes fall naturally — do not force them to any level. The item should feel authentic, not like an artificial exercise.
>
> **Style guidance**: produce content that could plausibly come from a real agent. Vary the voice, structure, and vocabulary across items. Use first-person where appropriate. Use technical terminology appropriate to the type.
>
> **Length guidance** by type:
> - decision: 200–600 words
> - spec: 300–1200 words
> - pr: 100–400 words
> - ticket: 50–250 words
> - component: 150–500 words
> - document: 400–1500 words
>
> Return ONLY the artifact content as markdown, with no meta-commentary, no frontmatter, and no explanation of your targeting decisions.
>
> Full HEAR rubric:
> [rubric content inserted here]

The generation script runs sequentially through all 50 items, writes each as a separate markdown file in `items/NNN-{type}-{hint}.md`, and commits the result.

---

## Quality control on generated items

After generation but before grading, a human spot-check validates:

1. All 50 files exist
2. Each file has the expected length range for its type
3. No file contains obvious generation artifacts ("here is an artifact that...", "note: this was designed to...")
4. A random sample of 10 items is read by the human for authenticity sanity check

If any of these fail, the affected items are regenerated with a more constrained prompt.

---

## Commit strategy

All 50 items are committed in a single git commit with the message:

> `docs(calibration): generate HEAR V1 calibration set (50 items)`

The commit includes:
- All 50 markdown files in `items/`
- This generation plan
- A short `items/README.md` describing the set

No further changes to items are allowed without a new version (v1.1, v2.0).

---

## Versioning

This is HEAR Calibration Generation Plan v1.0. Changes are versioned.
